name: William B. Williams
abstract: William B. Williams (August 6, 1923 – August 3, 1986) was an American disc jockey on New York City radio station WNEW for over four decades.

concerts.label: Afternoon at Carnegie Hall to Benefit Music for the Blind
Celebrity Tribute to Sammy Davis Jr.

Midnight Performance: Sammy Davis Jr., Singer

about_quotes.speaker: Catherine Russell
about_quotes.evidence: William B. Williams who was a DJ in New York who had, he would play what is now known as the Great American Songbook. But his program was called Make Believe Ballroom. And so we’d listen to those.
about_quotes.doc_title: Catherine Russell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 26, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: They said, “You have a contract and we are paying you all this money and you are following William B. Williams so you stay here.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Varro
about_quotes.evidence: William B. Williams especially in New York, WNEW had a lot of that stuff going on
about_quotes.doc_title: Johnny Varro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001
